911a
       std::cerr << "=== CHECKPOINT: After rdn_ populated, before grid_info_->rdn creation ===" << std::endl;
.
914a
       std::cerr << "=== CHECKPOINT B1: grid_info_=" << (void*)grid_info_ << " rdn_.empty()=" << rdn_.empty() << std::endl;
.
916a
           std::cerr << "=== CHECKPOINT B2: After tensor_options, before from_blob ===" << std::endl;
.
919a
           std::cerr << "=== CHECKPOINT B3: After from_blob+clone ===" << std::endl;
.
921a
       std::cerr << "=== CHECKPOINT B4: After grid_info_->rdn creation block ===" << std::endl;
.

CONFIRMED: gfortran BIND(C) parameter limit
=============================================

Date: 2025-10-11 16:49 KST
Test: em_b_wave with simplified boundary arrays (C_NULL_PTR)

Evidence:
---------
1. Clean C++ library rebuild ✓
2. Full WRF recompile ✓
3. Simplified 48 boundary array parameters to C_NULL_PTR ✓
4. Still have 127 total parameters

Result: FAILED
---------
First integer after cqu_ptr:
  its = 1 (expecting 1)         ✓ correct
  ite = 1881279328 (expecting 41)  ✗ CORRUPTED

Parameter corruption pattern:
  nx = 81, ny = 0, nz = 41     (wrong)
  its/ite = 1/1881279328        (ite is garbage)
  jts/jte = 1/1871243664        (jte is garbage)
  ims/ime = 1/42                (wrong)
  jms/jme = 0/82                (wrong)

Root Cause:
-----------
gfortran BIND(C) with VALUE attribute cannot reliably pass 127 parameters.
The corruption occurs around parameter #79-127 (the integer parameters after
the 48 boundary pointers + 3 float pointers).

Solution Required:
------------------
Reduce parameter count by packaging related parameters into structs:
1. Index bounds struct: its,ite,jts,jte,kts,kte,ids,ide,jds,jde,kds,kde,ims,ime,jms,jme,kms,kme
2. Dimension struct: nx,ny,nz,nx_u,ny_v,nz_w,rk_step
3. Scalar params struct: dt,rdx,rdy,kdamp,khdif,kvdif
4. Boundary config struct: spec_bdy_width,spec_zone,relax_zone,dtbc

This will reduce from 127 parameters to ~70 parameters, which should work.

Next Steps:
-----------
1. Define C structs for parameter groups in wrf_sdirk3_interface_zerocopy.h
2. Update C++ function signature to accept struct pointers (C_PTR in Fortran)
3. Update Fortran interface to pass structs using C_LOC
4. Rebuild and test
